case_title: FORCE TECHNOLOGY INTERNATIONAL PTY. LTD. VS. ALIPHCOM, INC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-14-2017 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF FORCE TECHNOLOGY INTERNATIONAL PTY. LTD.'S MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING ANSWER OF DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM INC FOR FAILURE TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY FOLLOWING COURT ORDER AND FOR SANCTIONS. NO APPEARANCES. THOSE WHO HAVE NOT MADE AN APPEARANCE ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT FINDS THAT ALIPHCOM, INC. FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S PRIOR DISCOVERY ORDER DATED JULY 14. THE COURT ORDERS AS A SANCTION THAT ALIPHCOM'S ANSWER BE STRICKEN. NO ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE ORDERED, AS THE NOTICE FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP SEC. 2023.040. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES GEERHART, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-10-2017 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge: Gail Dekreon, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768.

Robert E. Camors, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Force Technology International Pty. Ltd.
Teresa Jenkins, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant AliphCom, Inc.
Dismissal of DOES 1 to 50 was filed today, November 6, 2017.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief and the Declaration of Jack Minear, Managing Director for plaintiff Force Technology International Pty. Ltd., were filed today, November 6, 2017.
The Court took JUDICIAL NOTICE of the Orders filed in this matter prior to this morning's hearing.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on April 14, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Force Technology International Pty. Ltd., and against defendant AliphCom, Inc., as follows:

Damages for Breach of Contract - $1,661,726.65.
Damages for Negligent Interference with prospective economic relations - $673,917.00.
Prejudgment Interest - TBD.

Total - TBD.

Counsel shall prepare a Proposed Judgment, which shall include prejudgment interest, and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission, Set Two Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Robert E. Camors, Jr.
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Teresa Jenkins of Law Offices of Bob Camors 408-573-5744 appeared for plaintiff.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF FORCE TECHNOLOGY INTERNATIONAL PTY. LTD's Motion To Have Requests For Admission, Set Two Deemed admitted and for sanctions is GRANTED. Requests for admission Nos. 1-4 are deemed admitted. No opposition filed. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount $630.00, payment to be made within 20 calendar days. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Answer Of Defendant Aliphcom Inc For Failure To Respond To Discovery Following Court Order And For Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Declaration Of Robert E. Camors, Jr.
Off Calendar Aug-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF FORCE TECHNOLOGY INTERNATIONAL PTY. LTD.'S MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING ANSWER OF DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM INC FOR FAILURE TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY FOLLOWING COURT ORDER AND FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. Those who have not made an appearance are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted, no opposition filed. The Court finds that Aliphcom, Inc. failed to comply with the Court's prior discovery order dated July 14. The Court orders as a sanction that Aliphcom's Answer be stricken. No additional monetary sanctions are ordered, as the notice failed to comply with CCP sec. 2023.040. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted; To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And To Produce Documents, To Find That All Objections Have Been Waived, And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Robert Camors, Jr.
Off Calendar Jul-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Teresa Jenkins, of Law Office of Bob Camors, Tel. 408.573.5744, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. PLAINTIFF FORCE TECHNOLOGY INTERNATIONAL PTY. LTD. Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted; To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And To Produce Documents, To Find That All Objections Have Been Waived, And For Sanctions held on JUL-14-2017. No opposition filed. The document attached to Plaintiff's Request for Admission, Set One, entitled "Distributor Agreement" is deemed admitted. Defendant to serve responses to outstanding discovery without objections within 15 days after this order is filed. Defendant to pay Plaintiff sanctions in the amount of $760. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2017 Set For Trial On Nov-06-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
